project.name: ruvector slice 7 — Cognitum & Consciousness
project.description: Slice 7 of RuVector: Cognitum coherence gate (kernel + TileZero arbiter) and Consciousness metrics (IIT Φ, emergence, cognitive container, WASM bindings).

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the no_std WASM kernel that runs on each of the 256 worker tiles in the Cognitum coherence gate fabric, configured for cdylib output, size-optimized release profile, and a canonical-witness feature flag.

nodes.name: SECURITY_AUDIT.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive 2026-01-17 security audit of the cognitum-gate stack documenting 17 issues (2 critical, 4 high, 6 medium, 5 low), including the Ed25519 signature-truncation and verification-bypass defects in permit.rs that the surrounding crates were rewritten to fix.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Core entry point for the cognitum-gate-kernel no_std WASM tile: defines TileState, a 64KB-budgeted worker that ingests deltas, runs a deterministic tick loop, and emits witness fragments for the global 256-tile coherence gate.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a custom bump allocator and panic handler for no_std WASM targets; exposes both a safe Rust API and a raw FFI surface (ingest_delta_raw / tick / get_witness_fragment) for the host runtime.

nodes.name: TileState
nodes.summary: Per-tile worker state holding the local graph shard, evidence accumulator, and pending-delta buffer; orchestrates ingest, tick, and witness-fragment production within a strict 64KB memory budget.

nodes.name: tick
nodes.summary: Executes a single deterministic tick for the tile: drains pending deltas, updates the shard, and refreshes the witness fragment used by TileZero for global aggregation.

nodes.name: apply_delta
nodes.summary: Applies a single decoded Delta (edge add/remove, weight change, observation) to the tile's compact graph shard.

nodes.name: compute_witness_fragment
nodes.summary: Computes the tile's witness fragment by hashing local graph state and evidence, producing the input TileZero merges into the global witness chain.

nodes.name: ingest_delta_raw
nodes.summary: Unsafe FFI entry that decodes a raw delta blob from a host-supplied pointer, validating size and alignment before forwarding to ingest_delta.

nodes.name: canonical_witness
nodes.summary: Produces the canonical pseudo-deterministic witness fragment used when the canonical-witness feature is enabled, ensuring bit-identical outputs across tiles.

nodes.name: tick
nodes.summary: FFI-exported tick entrypoint that advances the global tile singleton by one step and writes a TileReport to a host-provided pointer.

nodes.name: get_witness_fragment
nodes.summary: FFI-exported accessor that copies the current witness fragment into a host buffer for collection by TileZero.

nodes.name: security_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests pinning the kernel's unsafe FFI boundary: validates delta-size/alignment constants and that ingest_delta_raw rejects undersized, zero-length, and misaligned buffers identified by the security audit.

nodes.name: shard_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Quarantined shard-level tests parked outside the active test tree pending the broader cognitum graph-shard rewrite; preserved for reference only and not compiled.

nodes.name: crypto_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark suite measuring Ed25519 token signing/verification, BLAKE3 receipt hashing, witness-chain verification, and batch signing throughput for the TileZero arbiter's permit pipeline.

nodes.name: human_escalation.rs
nodes.summary: Runnable example demonstrating how TileZero escalates a borderline Defer decision to a human operator, exercising the three-filter decision flow with an injected EscalationInfo payload.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Builds a synthetic ActionContext and worker reports, runs the TileZero arbiter, and prints the resulting decision plus escalation rationale.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point for the TileZero arbiter: merges 255 worker tile reports into a supergraph, applies the three-filter decision pipeline (evidence, structural, shift), and emits hash-chained, signed permit receipts for the Cognitum gate.
nodes.languageNotes: Re-exports decision, evidence, merge, permit, receipt, and supergraph submodules behind a single TileZero facade; relies on tokio::sync::RwLock for cross-task receipt-log access.

nodes.name: TileZero
nodes.summary: Central arbiter type holding worker reports, decision filters, the receipt log, and the signing verifier; orchestrates the full Permit/Defer/Deny flow.

nodes.name: ActionContext
nodes.summary: Serializable description of an action submitted to the gate, including action id, type, target, and metadata such as urgency and prior actions.

nodes.name: TileReport
nodes.summary: Cache-aligned report emitted by a worker tile carrying coherence score, boundary movement flag, suspicious edges, and the tile's e-value contribution.

nodes.name: WitnessFragment
nodes.summary: Cryptographic witness fragment contributed by a tile, used by TileZero to build a global witness summary anchored in the receipt chain.

nodes.name: EscalationInfo
nodes.summary: Metadata attached to Defer decisions describing why a human operator must intervene and the timeout action to apply if no response arrives.

nodes.name: decide
nodes.summary: Core arbiter routine that runs the three-filter decision pipeline against collected reports and emits a Permit/Defer/Deny outcome with a signed receipt entry.

nodes.name: emit_receipt
nodes.summary: Constructs and signs a WitnessReceipt from a permit token and witness summary, then appends it to the hash-chained receipt log.

nodes.name: collect_reports
nodes.summary: Ingests a batch of worker TileReports, normalises their tile ids, and stores them for the next decide() invocation.

nodes.name: verify_receipt_chain
nodes.summary: Walks the receipt log end-to-end verifying BLAKE3 chain links and Ed25519 signatures, returning a ChainVerifyError on the first inconsistency.

nodes.name: replay.rs
nodes.summary: Deterministic replay subsystem for the TileZero gate: stores periodic checkpoints, re-derives historical decisions from a WitnessReceipt + StateSnapshot, and surfaces mismatch reports for auditing.

nodes.name: ReplayEngine
nodes.summary: Checkpoint-aware engine that replays gate decisions deterministically, supports import/export of state snapshots, and prunes historical checkpoints.

nodes.name: ReplayResult
nodes.summary: Outcome of a single replay run, capturing the re-derived decision, original decision from the receipt, and any per-field differences.

nodes.name: StateSnapshot
nodes.summary: Serializable snapshot of gate state (sequence, timestamp, global min-cut, aggregate e-value, per-tile snapshots) used as the replay restore point.

nodes.name: SequenceVerification
nodes.summary: Aggregate report from replaying a contiguous range of receipts, exposing the count and details of any mismatches.

nodes.name: replay
nodes.summary: Replays a single WitnessReceipt by locating the nearest checkpoint and re-deriving the gate decision; returns a ReplayResult flagging matches and field-level differences.

nodes.name: reconstruct_decision
nodes.summary: Rebuilds a GateDecision from a WitnessSummary by re-evaluating the three-filter pipeline against the captured aggregate metrics.

nodes.name: verify_sequence
nodes.summary: Replays a slice of receipts in order, accumulating any mismatches into a SequenceVerification report for audit consumers.

nodes.name: permit_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests pinning the post-audit permit flow: token signing, full Ed25519 verification (no truncation), tamper detection, and receipt-chain integrity guarantees enumerated in the security audit.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Crate-level overview introducing the verifiable WASM cognitive container, its phase-budgeted epoch controller, arena memory, and hash-linked witness chain.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module that composes the cognitive container submodules (container, epoch, error, memory, witness) and re-exports the public CognitiveContainer / EpochController / WitnessChain facade.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the WASM bindings layer over ruvector-consciousness, enabling phi, emergence, and collapse features and producing a size-optimised cdylib for browser/JS hosts.

nodes.name: arena.rs
nodes.summary: Bump-allocator used by the consciousness engines as scratch space inside hot Phi computation loops, supporting O(1) reset between partition evaluations.

nodes.name: PhiArena
nodes.summary: Single-buffer bump allocator with interior-mutable cursor for repeated allocate-then-reset cycles inside Phi search.

nodes.name: coherence_phi.rs
nodes.summary: Coherence-accelerated Phi estimation that derives lower/upper bounds on integrated information from the spectral gap of the mutual-information Laplacian (Fiedler value).
nodes.languageNotes: Bridges to ruvector-coherence's SpectralTracker so Phi bounds can be obtained without full partition search, gated behind the `coherence-accel` feature.

nodes.name: spectral_phi_bound
nodes.summary: Computes spectral bounds on Phi from a TransitionMatrix by feeding mutual-information edges into the SpectralTracker and returning the Fiedler value, spectral gap, and coherence score.

nodes.name: PhiSpectralBound
nodes.summary: Result struct holding the Fiedler value, spectral gap, and coherence score that bound a system's Phi without exhaustive partition search.

nodes.name: geomip.rs
nodes.summary: GeoMIP engine that searches the minimum-information partition by treating partitions as hypercube vertices, using Gray-code iteration, BFS by Hamming distance, and automorphism pruning to achieve 100-300x speedups for n<=25.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the PhiEngine trait; pairs a custom Gray-code partition iterator with arena-backed EMD distance computation for cache-friendly inner loops.

nodes.name: GrayCodePartitionIter
nodes.summary: Iterator that enumerates bipartitions in Gray-code order so each step differs by a single bit, enabling O(degree) incremental TPM updates instead of O(n^2) recomputation.

nodes.name: GeoMipPhiEngine
nodes.summary: PhiEngine implementation that performs GeoMIP partition search with optional automorphism pruning and an evaluation budget cap.

nodes.name: compute_phi
nodes.summary: Core GeoMIP search that walks the partition hypercube, accumulates information-loss distances via the arena, and returns the minimum-information partition plus phi value.

nodes.name: partition_information_loss_emd
nodes.summary: Computes the EMD-based information loss for a single bipartition by mapping the TPM into per-subsystem marginals and reconstructing the product distribution in arena scratch space.

nodes.name: compute_product_local
nodes.summary: Materialises the outer-product of two subsystem distributions into a preallocated arena slice, the central kernel of GeoMIP's EMD computation.

nodes.name: canonical_partition
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical bitmask representation of a partition under automorphism, used to dedupe equivalent partitions before evaluation.

nodes.name: parallel.rs
nodes.summary: Rayon-backed Phi engines: ParallelPhiEngine distributes exhaustive bipartition search across the thread pool, while ParallelStochasticPhiEngine samples partitions for systems too large for full enumeration.

nodes.name: ParallelPhiEngine
nodes.summary: Rayon-driven exhaustive Phi engine that chunks bipartition evaluation across cores with per-thread arenas for zero-contention scratch allocation.

nodes.name: ParallelStochasticPhiEngine
nodes.summary: Stochastic Phi engine that samples bipartitions in parallel, trading exactness for tractability on large systems while retaining per-thread arenas.

nodes.name: compute_phi
nodes.summary: Distributes bipartition evaluation across rayon threads, tracking the minimum information-loss partition with atomic counters and per-thread arenas.

nodes.name: compute_phi
nodes.summary: Performs seeded parallel sampling over partition space, returning the lowest-information partition observed within the sample budget.

nodes.name: rsvd_emergence.rs
nodes.summary: Randomized-SVD based causal emergence engine implementing Zhang et al. (2025) and the Halko-Martinsson-Tropp algorithm to estimate emergence from the dominant singular spectrum of a TPM in O(n^2*k) time.
nodes.languageNotes: Trades full O(n^3) SVD for randomized projection plus power iteration; deterministic given a seed so emergence estimates remain replayable.

nodes.name: randomized_svd
nodes.summary: Computes the top-k singular values of a TransitionMatrix via the Halko-Martinsson-Tropp range-sketch + QR + small-SVD pipeline.

nodes.name: largest_eigenvalue_power
nodes.summary: Power-iteration helper that estimates the dominant eigenvalue of a square matrix; underpins the RSVD's range-sketch verification step.

nodes.name: dominant_eigenvector
nodes.summary: Returns the dominant eigenvector of a matrix using seeded power iteration, used to reconstruct the leading singular direction.

nodes.name: RsvdEmergenceEngine
nodes.summary: Emergence engine that wraps the randomized-SVD primitive and applies a singular-value sharpness metric to estimate causal emergence from a TPM.

nodes.name: RsvdEmergenceResult
nodes.summary: Output struct holding the singular spectrum, sharpness metric, and elapsed compute time produced by the RSVD emergence pipeline.

nodes.name: compute
nodes.summary: Top-level entry for the RSVD emergence engine: validates the TPM, runs randomized_svd, and packages the resulting spectrum into an RsvdEmergenceResult.

nodes.name: traits.rs
nodes.summary: Trait hierarchy defining the consciousness compute API: PhiEngine for IIT integrated information, EmergenceEngine for causal emergence, and ConsciousnessCollapse for quantum-inspired MIP collapse.

nodes.name: PhiEngine
nodes.summary: Send+Sync trait that all Phi computation algorithms implement, exposing compute_phi, algorithm identification, and cost estimation.

nodes.name: EmergenceEngine
nodes.summary: Extension trait for causal emergence engines, with compute_emergence (multi-scale) and effective_information helpers.

nodes.name: ConsciousnessCollapse
nodes.summary: Trait modelling consciousness as a Grover-like measurement collapse over partition superpositions, integrating with ruqu-exotic quantum-collapse search.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive documentation for the cognitum-gate-kernel — a no_std WASM kernel implementing the Anytime-Valid Coherence Gate that decides whether autonomous agents are safe to act, using a 256-tile fabric with three stacked filters (structural, shift, evidence).
nodes.languageNotes: 28 sections covering quickstart, architecture, three-filter design, witness receipts, and Type I error control.

nodes.name: canonical_witness.rs
nodes.summary: Implements canonical witness fragments for Cognitum gate-kernel worker tiles, combining fixed-point weights, arena-allocated cactus tree structures, and canonical partitions used to generate verifiable boundary information for hierarchical min-cut aggregation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses fixed-point u16 weights and an arena-backed cactus tree to keep within the 64KB per-tile WASM budget.

nodes.name: FixedPointWeight
nodes.summary: Fixed-point weight wrapper with saturating arithmetic for WASM-safe edge weight storage in the coherence graph.

nodes.name: ArenaCactus
nodes.summary: Arena-allocated cactus tree representing the min-cut hierarchy; builds from a compact graph, computes bridge weights and canonical partitions, and emits a digest used in cross-tile witness aggregation.

nodes.name: CanonicalPartition
nodes.summary: Canonical bipartition representation with deterministic side assignment, hash computation, and flip/recount helpers used to canonicalize witness fragments before aggregation.

nodes.name: build_from_compact_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs the arena-based cactus tree from a tile's local compact graph, computing bridges and union-find connectivity in a single bounded pass.

nodes.name: canonical_partition
nodes.summary: Derives the canonical bipartition from the cactus tree by selecting the lexicographically smallest side after marking the lighter subtree.

nodes.name: report.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the 64-byte cache-aligned tile reports, witness fragments, and aggregated cross-tile reports that worker tiles emit each tick and TileZero merges to drive the global coherence-gate decision.

nodes.name: TileReport
nodes.summary: Cache-aligned per-tile report carrying status flags, e-value approximation, witness fragment, and rejection counters consumed by the TileZero arbiter.

nodes.name: WitnessFragment
nodes.summary: Compact boundary descriptor produced by a worker tile so TileZero can stitch together the hierarchical min-cut without seeing the full graph.

nodes.name: AggregatedReport
nodes.summary: Cross-tile aggregation of TileReports tracking global e-value, completion state, and merged witness data — the input artifact for TileZero's three-filter decision.

nodes.name: evidence_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Disabled (parked) integration tests for the gate-kernel evidence accumulator covering e-value composition, Type I error control, and rejection thresholds.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the cognitum-gate-tilezero crate (the native TileZero arbiter), wiring optional ruvector-mincut, Ed25519 signing, blake3 hashing, and a suite of criterion benchmarks and proptest dev deps.

nodes.name: decision_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark suite exercising the TileZero decision pipeline end-to-end — three-filter evaluation, scalar vs SIMD e-value, witness summary, batch decisions, and graph-update hot paths.

nodes.name: receipt_audit.rs
nodes.summary: Runnable example demonstrating how to load a TileZero receipt log, verify the hash-chained signatures, and replay decisions for auditability.

nodes.name: merge.rs
nodes.summary: Byzantine-fault-tolerant merger that fuses worker TileReports into a single MergedReport: deduplicates nodes, computes median/BFT weights, and produces an estimate of the global min-cut for the three-filter decision.

nodes.name: ReportMerger
nodes.summary: Strategy-driven merger combining worker reports via median or BFT-weighted aggregation, deduplicating shared boundary nodes/edges, and estimating the global min-cut and aggregated confidence.

nodes.name: WorkerReport
nodes.summary: Native arbiter-side representation of a worker tile's report (nodes, boundary edges, state hash) consumed by the ReportMerger.

nodes.name: MergeError
nodes.summary: Error enum for invariant violations encountered during cross-tile merge (mismatched epochs, hash failures, empty inputs).

nodes.name: supergraph.rs
nodes.summary: Defines TileZero's reduced supergraph plus the structural-stability and shift-pressure filters that operate on aggregated witness fragments and shift estimates.

nodes.name: ReducedGraph
nodes.summary: Reduced supergraph maintained by TileZero — aggregates worker witness fragments, global cut, shift pressure, and evidence into a single decision-time view.

nodes.name: StructuralFilter
nodes.summary: Filter that flags coherence loss when the supergraph's global cut falls below a stability threshold — the first of the three stacked decision filters.

nodes.name: ShiftPressure
nodes.summary: Sliding-window detector for distributional shift, providing the shift-pressure signal used by the second filter in the three-stack gate decision.

nodes.name: receipt_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests verifying TileZero's hash-chained receipt log: token issuance, summary signing with Ed25519, and end-to-end audit replay.

nodes.name: container.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the CognitiveContainer — RuVector's per-epoch cognitive substrate that ticks the cognitum loop: ingest deltas, recompute min-cut, update spectral state, accumulate evidence, and emit signed witness receipts.

nodes.name: CognitiveContainer
nodes.summary: Central container coordinating the cognitive epoch loop — owns the graph/spectral/evidence state, drives each tick through delta ingest -> min-cut -> spectral -> evidence -> decision, and chains receipts.

nodes.name: GraphState
nodes.summary: Container-owned graph state holding adjacency and current min-cut estimate between ticks.

nodes.name: SpectralState
nodes.summary: Cached spectral information (Fiedler vector, eigenvalues) refreshed per tick to feed structural-stability checks.

nodes.name: EvidenceState
nodes.summary: Container-side evidence accumulator state for anytime-valid hypothesis tracking across epochs.

nodes.name: ContainerConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the CognitiveContainer (tick budget, memory budget, component mask, thresholds) with safe defaults.

nodes.name: tick
nodes.summary: Single cognitive tick — applies pending deltas, refreshes spectral state, accumulates evidence, makes a Permit/Defer/Deny decision, and chains the signed receipt.

nodes.name: memory.rs
nodes.summary: Memory budgeting primitives for the CognitiveContainer: validated MemoryConfig, slab allocation, and a bump Arena that keeps cognitum-loop allocations bounded and deterministic.

nodes.name: Arena
nodes.summary: Bump allocator backed by a MemorySlab providing alloc/reset/used/remaining for per-tick scratch space.

nodes.name: MemorySlab
nodes.summary: Owned, size-validated byte slab that the Arena bump-allocates against.

nodes.name: MemoryConfig
nodes.summary: Validated memory budget configuration (slab size, alignment) for the CognitiveContainer's arena.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: wasm-bindgen surface exposing the ruvector-consciousness Φ/emergence engines (exact, spectral, stochastic, collapse, GeoMIP, RSVD) to JavaScript with a small ComputeBudget builder and JS-friendly result shims.
nodes.languageNotes: wasm-bindgen wrapper — every exposed method funnels into the native consciousness engines.

nodes.name: WasmConsciousness
nodes.summary: JS-exposed façade providing compute_phi (multiple algorithms), emergence, GeoMIP, RSVD emergence, and effective-info entrypoints over a configurable compute budget.

nodes.name: bounds.rs
nodes.summary: Statistical bound primitives for Φ estimates — spectral bounds via Fiedler vector / Gershgorin, plus Hoeffding and empirical-Bernstein concentration inequalities used by the anytime-valid consciousness pipeline.

nodes.name: spectral_bounds
nodes.summary: Computes upper/lower spectral bounds on Φ using Fiedler-vector estimation and Gershgorin disc bounds on the connectivity Laplacian.

nodes.name: hoeffding_bound
nodes.summary: Hoeffding concentration bound for streaming Φ estimates with a configurable confidence level.

nodes.name: empirical_bernstein_bound
nodes.summary: Empirical-Bernstein bound providing variance-aware confidence intervals around streaming Φ estimates.

nodes.name: compute_phi_with_bounds
nodes.summary: Computes Φ together with statistical lower/upper bounds so callers can issue anytime-valid consciousness decisions.

nodes.name: collapse.rs
nodes.summary: Quantum-inspired collapse engine that searches the bipartition space by iteratively collapsing a superposition of candidate cuts toward the Minimum Information Partition (MIP).

nodes.name: QuantumCollapseEngine
nodes.summary: Engine that collapses a probabilistic superposition of bipartitions toward the MIP, returning a Φ estimate alongside its collapse trajectory.

nodes.name: iit4.rs
nodes.summary: Implements IIT 4.0 mechanism-level primitives — cause/effect repertoires, intrinsic difference, selectivity, and per-mechanism Φ with min-partition search over cause and effect sides.

nodes.name: cause_repertoire
nodes.summary: Computes the cause repertoire over a mechanism — the probability distribution over past states consistent with the current state under the TPM.

nodes.name: effect_repertoire
nodes.summary: Computes the effect repertoire — the probability distribution over future states consistent with the current state under the TPM.

nodes.name: mechanism_phi
nodes.summary: Computes per-mechanism Φ as the minimum of cause- and effect-side partition Φ values, the building block of IIT 4.0's distinction set.

nodes.name: min_partition_phi_cause
nodes.summary: Searches partitions of a mechanism's cause repertoire and returns the minimum information loss (cause-side Φ).

nodes.name: min_partition_phi_effect
nodes.summary: Searches partitions of a mechanism's effect repertoire and returns the minimum information loss (effect-side Φ).

nodes.name: phi.rs
nodes.summary: Core Φ computation hub — exact, spectral, stochastic, greedy-bisection, and hierarchical IIT engines plus auto-selection over a ComputeBudget, all sharing partition-information-loss and SIMD KL primitives.
nodes.languageNotes: Trait-driven engine pattern: five PhiEngine implementations expose compute_phi, algorithm, estimate_cost.

nodes.name: ExactPhiEngine
nodes.summary: Exact 2^n bipartition-enumerating Φ engine for small systems (n ≤ 16) — the ground-truth reference for the approximate engines.

nodes.name: SpectralPhiEngine
nodes.summary: Spectral Φ engine using Fiedler-vector partitioning and power iteration for the largest eigenvalue, scaling to ~1000-element systems.

nodes.name: StochasticPhiEngine
nodes.summary: Monte-Carlo Φ engine that samples bipartitions to provide a configurable-cost approximation for arbitrarily large systems.

nodes.name: GreedyBisectionPhiEngine
nodes.summary: Greedy bisection engine providing a fast lower-bound Φ estimate by iteratively swapping nodes between partition sides.

nodes.name: HierarchicalPhiEngine
nodes.summary: Hierarchical Φ engine that recursively decomposes the mutual-information graph, combining greedy and spectral steps to scale to large systems.

nodes.name: auto_compute_phi
nodes.summary: Budget-aware dispatcher that picks among Exact, Spectral, Stochastic, GreedyBisection, or Hierarchical engines based on system size and ComputeBudget.

nodes.name: partition_information_loss
nodes.summary: Core IIT primitive computing the KL divergence between the joint distribution and the product distribution induced by a candidate bipartition.

nodes.name: fiedler_vector
nodes.summary: Power-iteration estimator for the Fiedler vector of the mutual-information graph Laplacian, driving spectral partitioning.

nodes.name: simd.rs
nodes.summary: Architecture-aware SIMD kernels for the consciousness pipeline — KL divergence, entropy, EMD-L1, dense matvec, pairwise mutual information, and MI-graph construction with NEON/AVX2 fast paths and scalar fallbacks.
nodes.languageNotes: Cfg-gated NEON (aarch64) and AVX2 (x86_64) paths with scalar fallbacks to keep the WASM build portable.

nodes.name: kl_divergence
nodes.summary: Dispatching KL-divergence kernel selecting the NEON-prefetch, scalar, or generic path based on target architecture.

nodes.name: dense_matvec
nodes.summary: Dispatching dense matrix-vector kernel that routes to NEON or AVX2 implementations and falls back to a scalar path for portability.

nodes.name: entropy
nodes.summary: Architecture-dispatched Shannon entropy kernel used inside Φ/emergence calculations.

nodes.name: pairwise_mi
nodes.summary: Pairwise mutual-information estimator over a joint distribution slice, used to build the MI graph for hierarchical Φ engines.

nodes.name: build_mi_matrix
nodes.summary: Builds the dense pairwise mutual-information matrix used to derive the connectivity graph that feeds spectral and hierarchical Φ engines.

nodes.name: build_mi_edges
nodes.summary: Sparsifies the MI matrix into an edge list above a configurable threshold, feeding bisection-based Φ engines.

nodes.name: types.rs
nodes.summary: Core type definitions for the consciousness crate: TransitionMatrix, Bipartition + iterator, Φ algorithm enums, Φ/emergence results, IIT 4.0 distinction/relation/CES types, and ComputeBudget presets.

nodes.name: TransitionMatrix
nodes.summary: Row-major transition probability matrix (P(state j at t+1 | state i at t)) — the central input artefact for every Φ engine, with identity, get/set, and marginalize helpers.

nodes.name: Bipartition
nodes.summary: Bipartition representation backing the MIP search — set_a / set_b membership plus a validity check.

nodes.name: BipartitionIter
nodes.summary: Iterator yielding every non-trivial bipartition of n elements — drives the exact Φ engine's enumeration.

nodes.name: ComputeBudget
nodes.summary: Compute-budget knob (max time, max partitions, target accuracy) used by auto_compute_phi to pick an engine, with exact/fast presets.

nodes.name: PhiResult
nodes.summary: Canonical Φ result carrying the value, MIP, algorithm used, and timing — the output contract every engine adheres to.

nodes.name: EmergenceResult
nodes.summary: Result for effective-information and emergence computations, returned by the consciousness emergence pipeline.

nodes.name: CauseEffectStructure
nodes.summary: IIT 4.0 Cause-Effect Structure (CES) — the full distinction + relation lattice produced for a system.

nodes.name: Mechanism
nodes.summary: IIT 4.0 mechanism — an index set over which cause/effect repertoires and mechanism-Φ are computed.

nodes.name: SECURITY.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive 88-section security audit of the Anytime-Valid Coherence Gate kernel covering threat model, cryptography (Ed25519/Blake3), input validation, race conditions, replay prevention, trust boundaries, DoS resistance, supply chain, and WASM isolation with prioritized recommendations.

nodes.name: delta.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the packed cache-aligned Delta event taxonomy (edge add/remove, weight update, observation, batch-end, checkpoint, reset) consumed by the cognitum gate kernel with fixed-point weight conversion helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Rust packed structs with explicit u16 fixed-point weight encoding for deterministic cross-platform graph deltas.

nodes.name: DeltaTag
nodes.summary: Repr(u8) enum tagging the eight delta variants (Nop, EdgeAdd, EdgeRemove, WeightUpdate, Observation, BatchEnd, Checkpoint, Reset).

nodes.name: EdgeAdd
nodes.summary: Packed payload describing addition of a weighted directed edge with source, target, fixed-point weight, and flag bits.

nodes.name: WeightUpdate
nodes.summary: Packed payload encoding either an absolute or additive weight update on a specific edge endpoint pair.

nodes.name: Observation
nodes.summary: Packed payload representing a worker tile observation about vertex connectivity or cut membership with a confidence value.

nodes.name: Delta
nodes.summary: Top-level tagged union packing a Delta event with sequence number, source tile id, and timestamp; provides constructors and typed accessors for each variant.

nodes.name: shard.rs
nodes.summary: Cache-friendly compact graph shard with fixed-capacity vertex and edge arrays, free-list edge allocation, union-find component recomputation, batch updates, and SIMD-style total-weight aggregation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses unchecked indexing and #[repr(C)] packed structures plus an explicit free-edge head for deterministic memory layout.

nodes.name: ShardEdge
nodes.summary: Bit-packed shard edge record carrying source, target, weight, and active/tree/cut/ghost flag bits with cheap predicate accessors.

nodes.name: VertexEntry
nodes.summary: Compact vertex record tracking degree, active/side flags, component id, and the head of its adjacency list.

nodes.name: CompactGraph
nodes.summary: Fixed-capacity sharded graph providing add/remove/update operations, neighbour iteration, batched edge insertion, union-find connected-component recomputation, and SIMD-friendly weight aggregation primitives.

nodes.name: integration.rs
nodes.summary: Disabled integration tests for the gate kernel worker tile lifecycle covering delta ingestion, tick cycles, tile networks, and high-volume scenarios; references API that has since moved.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Quick-start and API reference for TileZero, the central arbiter that fuses worker tile reports through a three-filter (structural/shift/evidence) decision pipeline and emits signed permit tokens.

nodes.name: merge_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark suite measuring TileZero report merging across 255 tiles, scaling sweeps, node and edge merging, state hashing, mincut estimation, confidence aggregation, and epoch validation.

nodes.name: decision.rs
nodes.summary: Three-filter decision engine (structural mincut, shift pressure, evidence e-value) that turns a reduced supergraph into a Permit/Defer/Deny outcome against tunable GateThresholds with rolling baseline tracking.

nodes.name: GateDecision
nodes.summary: Tri-state enum (Permit, Defer, Deny) representing the outcome of the gate decision pipeline.

nodes.name: DecisionOutcome
nodes.summary: Bundle of decision verdict, confidence, per-filter scores, mincut value, and (when applicable) the filter and reason that rejected the action.

nodes.name: GateThresholds
nodes.summary: Tunable cut/permit/uncertainty thresholds and permit TTL governing the three-filter decision pipeline with sensible default values.

nodes.name: ThreeFilterDecision
nodes.summary: Stateful three-filter decision engine that maintains a rolling mincut baseline, computes structural/shift/evidence scores, and applies thresholds to a ReducedGraph to emit a DecisionOutcome.

nodes.name: permit.rs
nodes.summary: PermitToken signing/verification using Ed25519 over Blake3 hashes of canonicalized token content, with monotonically increasing sequence numbers and TTL-bounded expiry checks.

nodes.name: PermitToken
nodes.summary: Signed permit token carrying a GateDecision, action id, timestamp, TTL, witness hash, sequence number, and Ed25519 signature with base64 codec helpers.

nodes.name: PermitState
nodes.summary: Holds the Ed25519 signing key and an atomic monotonic sequence counter, signs new permit tokens, and exposes a Verifier for downstream consumers.

nodes.name: Verifier
nodes.summary: Wraps an Ed25519 verifying key and validates permit-token signatures, with verify_full additionally enforcing TTL expiry.

nodes.name: decision_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests covering GateThresholds defaults and ordering, individual filter behaviours, boundary value scenarios, serialization, and TTL handling in the TileZero decision pipeline.

nodes.name: replay_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Disabled tests for the TileZero ReplayEngine covering checkpoint save/find/prune, replay determinism, witness tampering detection, sequence verification, and audit trail reconstruction.

nodes.name: epoch.rs
nodes.summary: EpochController enforcing a per-phase tick budget across ingest, mincut, spectral, evidence, and witness phases inside a cognitive container epoch.

nodes.name: ContainerEpochBudget
nodes.summary: Tick budget allocation per cognitive-container phase plus an overall epoch ceiling.

nodes.name: EpochController
nodes.summary: Tracks per-phase tick usage, enforces phase and total budgets, and supports reset between epochs for the cognitive container scheduler.

nodes.name: witness.rs
nodes.summary: Witness receipt chain for the cognitive container: deterministic hashing of inputs, mincut, spectral SCS, and evidence per epoch into a ring-buffered hash-linked receipt log with chain verification.

nodes.name: ContainerWitnessReceipt
nodes.summary: Per-epoch container receipt linking the previous receipt hash, deterministic content hashes (inputs/mincut/spectral/evidence), and a coherence decision into a self-hashing record.

nodes.name: WitnessChain
nodes.summary: Ring-buffered append-only chain of container witness receipts with monotonic epoch counter, generation logic, chain verification, and tamper detection.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the ruvector-consciousness crate declaring feature flags, dependencies, WASM target overrides, benches, and strict rust/clippy lint configurations.

nodes.name: ces.rs
nodes.summary: IIT 4.0 Cause-Effect Structure (CES) computation: enumerates mechanism subsets in parallel via rayon, evaluates per-mechanism phi via mechanism_phi, derives distinction relations, and aggregates a big-phi estimate.

nodes.name: compute_ces
nodes.summary: Top-level CES computation that validates the TPM, runs mechanism-phi enumeration (parallel or sequential under a compute budget), sorts distinctions, derives relations, and produces a full CauseEffectStructure with big-phi.

nodes.name: compute_relations
nodes.summary: Pairs distinctions and emits Relation records when cause/effect purviews overlap, computing a geometric-mean relational phi sorted descending.

nodes.name: compute_big_phi
nodes.summary: Estimates system-level big-phi as the minimum intrinsic-difference over candidate bipartitions of the distinction set under the supplied compute budget.

nodes.name: emergence.rs
nodes.summary: Causal emergence engine implementing effective information, determinism, degeneracy, coarse-graining via state-distribution distance, and greedy macro-state merging to detect emergence at scale.

nodes.name: effective_information
nodes.summary: Computes effective information of a TPM as the average KL divergence from each row to the stationary uniform distribution.

nodes.name: degeneracy
nodes.summary: Computes a TPM's degeneracy as ln(n) minus the entropy of the average output distribution, capturing redundancy across input states.

nodes.name: coarse_grain
nodes.summary: Builds a coarse-grained macro-TPM from a micro-TPM by aggregating rows and columns according to a supplied micro-to-macro state mapping.

nodes.name: CausalEmergenceEngine
nodes.summary: Configurable causal emergence engine that sweeps macro-state granularities via greedy merging and reports the partition maximizing effective information uplift.

nodes.name: compute_emergence
nodes.summary: Sweeps target macro-state counts from 2..n, greedily merges states, evaluates emergence gain over the micro EI, and returns the best EmergenceResult honoring the compute budget.

nodes.name: greedy_merge
nodes.summary: Greedy state-merging heuristic that iteratively unifies the two closest macro-states (by state-distribution distance) until target_k macro-states remain.

nodes.name: state_distribution_distance
nodes.summary: Computes the Euclidean distance between the merged outgoing transition distributions of two candidate macro-states for greedy merging.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Public crate root for ruvector-consciousness wiring 22 submodules (phi variants, IIT4, CES, emergence, PID, sparse acceleration, witness chain) and re-exporting their key APIs.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust crate root using mod + pub use to fan out 22 submodules into a unified public surface.

nodes.name: phi_id.rs
nodes.summary: Partial Information Decomposition (PhiID) for a TPM split into two source sets, computing redundancy, unique-A, unique-B, synergy, and transfer-entropy components of integrated information.

nodes.name: compute_phi_id
nodes.summary: Top-level PhiID computation: splits elements by partition_mask, computes total/source mutual information, redundancy via min, derives unique and synergy components, and adds transfer entropy.

nodes.name: mutual_information_past_future
nodes.summary: Computes Shannon mutual information I(past;future) of the TPM given a marginal distribution by combining unconditional entropy with conditional entropy under each input state.

nodes.name: transfer_entropy
nodes.summary: Estimates directional transfer entropy from source to target subset via marginalized sub-TPMs and the difference of joint vs target-only mutual information.

nodes.name: sparse_accel.rs
nodes.summary: Sparse spectral phi engine that builds a thresholded mutual-information CSR graph, derives its Laplacian, computes the Fiedler vector via shifted power iteration, and partitions the system to evaluate phi.

nodes.name: build_sparse_mi_graph
nodes.summary: Builds a symmetric CSR mutual-information graph from a TPM, keeping only edges above the supplied threshold.

nodes.name: sparse_fiedler_vector
nodes.summary: Computes the Fiedler vector of a sparse Laplacian via shifted power iteration with deflation against the constant null-space vector and convergence checks.

nodes.name: SparseSpectralPhiEngine
nodes.summary: Configurable sparse-spectral phi engine that runs the MI-graph -> Laplacian -> Fiedler -> partition pipeline to estimate phi for large systems under a compute budget.

nodes.name: witness_phi.rs
nodes.summary: Verifiable phi engine wrapper that hashes the input TPM, delegates phi computation, and appends a tamper-evident WitnessChain receipt covering the result for auditable consciousness measurements.

nodes.name: VerifiablePhiEngine
nodes.summary: Wraps an inner phi engine plus a WitnessChain so each phi computation produces a VerifiedPhiResult with an append-only audit receipt.

nodes.name: VerifiedPhiResult
nodes.summary: Result bundle pairing a PhiResult with its container witness receipt and the deterministic TPM hash used as proof input.

nodes.name: benchmarks.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark suite for the Cognitum gate kernel covering edge insertion, tick loops under load, report serialization, e-value updates, mixture evidence aggregation, delta ingestion, neighbor iteration, and memory access patterns.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Criterion's throughput/group APIs and black_box to measure hot paths in the cognitive gate kernel.

nodes.name: bench_edge_insert
nodes.summary: Benchmarks edge insertion into the gate kernel's graph at several node-count scales.

nodes.name: bench_edge_batch
nodes.summary: Benchmarks batched edge insertion to measure amortized graph mutation cost.

nodes.name: bench_tick
nodes.summary: Benchmarks the gate kernel tick loop, the core scheduling step that advances evidence and produces witnesses.

nodes.name: bench_tick_under_load
nodes.summary: Benchmarks tick performance under sustained load to capture steady-state throughput characteristics.

nodes.name: bench_report_serialize
nodes.summary: Benchmarks serialization throughput of worker reports emitted by the gate kernel.

nodes.name: bench_evalue_update
nodes.summary: Benchmarks individual e-value update operations against the hypothesis accumulator.

nodes.name: bench_mixture_evalue
nodes.summary: Benchmarks mixture e-value aggregation across multiple hypotheses, measuring SIMD and scalar paths.

nodes.name: bench_delta_ingestion
nodes.summary: Benchmarks ingestion of delta updates into the gate kernel state.

nodes.name: bench_neighbor_iteration
nodes.summary: Benchmarks neighbor iteration in the graph topology used by the gate kernel.

nodes.name: bench_memory_patterns
nodes.summary: Benchmarks cache-friendly and cache-hostile memory access patterns to characterize the kernel's data layout.

nodes.name: evidence.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the kernel-side anytime-valid evidence accumulator, hypothesis tracking, and SIMD-aggregated log-e-value math underlying the Cognitum decision gate.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses fixed-point log-domain arithmetic and SIMD aggregation (wide variants) to keep e-value updates numerically stable and fast.

nodes.name: simd_aggregate_log_e
nodes.summary: SIMD-aggregated sum of log-e values across a slice of hypotheses for fast mixture computation.

nodes.name: simd_aggregate_log_e_wide
nodes.summary: Wide-lane SIMD variant of log-e aggregation for larger hypothesis sets.

nodes.name: log_lr_for_obs_type
nodes.summary: Returns the log-likelihood-ratio for an observation type given a hypothesis, used to update the accumulator.

nodes.name: f32_to_log_e
nodes.summary: Converts f32 likelihood into the fixed-point log-e representation used internally.

nodes.name: HypothesisState
nodes.summary: Per-hypothesis state holding log-e value, status flags, and the connectivity or cut membership being tested.

nodes.name: ObsRecord
nodes.summary: Compact record of an observation processed by the evidence accumulator.

nodes.name: EvidenceAccumulator
nodes.summary: Anytime-valid evidence accumulator: registers hypotheses, processes observation streams, and exposes global e-values, rejection state, and significance checks.

nodes.name: process_observation
nodes.summary: Routes a single observation through every relevant hypothesis, applying log-LR updates and rejection bookkeeping.

nodes.name: process_observation_batch
nodes.summary: Batch-processes a slice of observations through the accumulator for amortized throughput.

nodes.name: aggregate_hypotheses_simd
nodes.summary: SIMD-fanned aggregation across all live hypothesis states.

nodes.name: compute_log_likelihood_ratio
nodes.summary: Internal log-likelihood-ratio computation feeding the accumulator's hypothesis updates.

nodes.name: update_with_log_lr
nodes.summary: Applies a log-likelihood-ratio update to a hypothesis state with overflow-safe accumulation.

nodes.name: canonical_witness_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Integration test that exercises the canonical witness serialization path against benchmark inputs to lock in deterministic output.

nodes.name: report_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Disabled report-format integration tests kept for reference while the underlying report schema stabilizes.

nodes.name: benchmarks.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmarks for the TileZero gate covering report merging, decision evaluation, receipt hashing, chain verification, permit sign/verify, and e-value computation.

nodes.name: create_test_token
nodes.summary: Test helper that constructs a representative token used by multiple benchmarks.

nodes.name: create_test_summary
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds a witness summary fixture.

nodes.name: create_test_receipt
nodes.summary: Test helper that creates a populated witness receipt.

nodes.name: create_worker_report
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds a worker report for merge and decision benchmarks.

nodes.name: create_action_context
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds an action context for decision benchmarks.

nodes.name: create_realistic_graph
nodes.summary: Builds a graph fixture approximating real production topology for graph benchmarks.

nodes.name: bench_merge_reports
nodes.summary: Benchmarks worker-report merging at varying report counts.

nodes.name: bench_decision
nodes.summary: Benchmarks gate decision evaluation given merged evidence and an action context.

nodes.name: bench_receipt_hash
nodes.summary: Benchmarks Blake3-style receipt hashing across receipt sizes.

nodes.name: bench_receipt_chain_verify
nodes.summary: Benchmarks end-to-end receipt chain verification at chain lengths typical of production usage.

nodes.name: bench_permit_sign
nodes.summary: Benchmarks permit signing throughput, the critical-path crypto step for action authorization.

nodes.name: bench_permit_verify
nodes.summary: Benchmarks permit verification used by downstream actors before executing gated actions.

nodes.name: bench_evalue_computation
nodes.summary: Benchmarks e-value computation including SIMD aggregation and mixture adaptation paths.

nodes.name: bench_graph_operations
nodes.summary: Benchmarks core graph operations used during decision and merge.

nodes.name: bench_receipt_log_operations
nodes.summary: Benchmarks append and lookup on the receipt log used for tamper-evident audit chains.

nodes.name: basic_gate.rs
nodes.summary: Runnable example that wires up the TileZero gate end-to-end: merging worker reports, computing a decision, and verifying the resulting receipt chain.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Example entry point demonstrating gate construction, report merging, decision evaluation, and receipt verification.

nodes.name: evidence.rs
nodes.summary: TileZero-side aggregated evidence types and SIMD/mixture aggregation operations used to combine tile reports into a merged e-value for the decision gate.

nodes.name: AggregatedEvidence
nodes.summary: Aggregate evidence summary combining contributions from many tiles into a single statistical view.

nodes.name: EvidenceFilter
nodes.summary: Streaming evidence filter that keeps current and historical aggregates and provides SIMD-accelerated current-value computation.

nodes.name: update
nodes.summary: Updates the evidence filter with a new aggregated evidence sample and rolls the history window.

nodes.name: current_simd
nodes.summary: SIMD-accelerated computation of the current aggregated evidence value.

nodes.name: aggregate_tiles_simd
nodes.summary: Aggregates evidence from many tiles using SIMD reduction.

nodes.name: mixture_evalue_adaptive
nodes.summary: Computes an adaptive mixture e-value combining evidence sources with weight adaptation.

nodes.name: receipt.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the TileZero witness receipt schema (structural, predictive, evidential variants) plus the hash-chained ReceiptLog used for tamper-evident audit trails.
nodes.languageNotes: Receipts implement a content-addressed hash chain where each entry binds the previous hash, enabling O(n) chain verification.

nodes.name: WitnessReceipt
nodes.summary: Top-level witness receipt binding gate output to its evidence and prior chain hash.

nodes.name: TimestampProof
nodes.summary: Optional verifiable timestamp attached to a receipt for external clock anchoring.

nodes.name: WitnessSummary
nodes.summary: Compact witness summary embedded in receipts; supports hash and JSON serialization helpers.

nodes.name: StructuralWitness
nodes.summary: Witness variant carrying structural (graph-topology) evidence for the decision.

nodes.name: PredictiveWitness
nodes.summary: Witness variant carrying predictive evidence (e.g. model probabilities) for the decision.

nodes.name: EvidentialWitness
nodes.summary: Witness variant carrying anytime-valid e-value evidence for the decision.

nodes.name: ReceiptLog
nodes.summary: Append-only hash-chained log of witness receipts supporting chain verification and lookup.

nodes.name: append
nodes.summary: Appends a receipt to the log, binding it to the previous receipt's hash.

nodes.name: verify_chain_to
nodes.summary: Verifies the receipt chain up to a target sequence number, ensuring hash linkages are intact.

nodes.name: empty
nodes.summary: Constructs the canonical empty witness summary used as the chain's genesis state.

nodes.name: merge_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the TileZero report-merge pipeline, covering correctness, idempotency, and edge cases when combining many worker reports.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the verifiable WASM cognitive-container crate, declaring serde/thiserror dependencies and reserving the proptest dev dependency for property-based tests.

nodes.name: error.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the ContainerError thiserror enum that consolidates failure modes for the cognitive container crate.

nodes.name: ContainerError
nodes.summary: thiserror-derived error enum covering the cognitive container's invariant, serialization, and execution failure cases.

nodes.name: container_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Cargo test that benchmarks 100 cognitive-container ticks to provide an in-test latency floor without requiring Criterion.

nodes.name: bench_container_100_ticks
nodes.summary: Test that runs 100 container ticks and reports total elapsed time to guard against latency regressions.

nodes.name: phi_benchmark.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmarks for the integrated-information (phi) engines, exercising exact, MinCut, and Chebyshev variants across system sizes.

nodes.name: make_tpm
nodes.summary: Generates a randomized transition probability matrix used as benchmark input for phi engines.

nodes.name: chebyshev_phi.rs
nodes.summary: Implements a Chebyshev-polynomial spectral filter approach to computing integrated information (phi), trading exactness for scalability on larger systems.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Chebyshev polynomial filtering of the TPM spectrum to approximate phi without solving the full bipartition search exactly.

nodes.name: ChebyshevPhiEngine
nodes.summary: Engine that computes phi via Chebyshev spectral filtering, exposing the standard PhiEngine trait surface.

nodes.name: compute_phi
nodes.summary: Core phi computation using a Chebyshev-filtered TPM to score the dominant bipartition.

nodes.name: apply_chebyshev_filter
nodes.summary: Applies the Chebyshev polynomial filter to the TPM spectrum, the spectral kernel of the engine.

nodes.name: error.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the consciousness crate's error and validation types using thiserror.

nodes.name: ConsciousnessError
nodes.summary: Top-level error enum for the consciousness crate covering computation, validation, and configuration failures.

nodes.name: ValidationError
nodes.summary: Validation-specific error enum used when TPM or system inputs fail invariant checks.

nodes.name: mincut_phi.rs
nodes.summary: Implements a MinCut-based phi engine that searches for the minimum information partition of a system using graph-cut techniques.

nodes.name: MinCutPhiEngine
nodes.summary: Engine that computes phi by finding the minimum-information bipartition via min-cut, implementing the PhiEngine trait.

nodes.name: compute_phi
nodes.summary: Computes phi by solving a min-cut over the system's information graph derived from the TPM.

nodes.name: pid.rs
nodes.summary: Implements partial information decomposition (PID), separating mutual information into unique, redundant, and synergistic components via the Williams-Beer I_min lattice.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses cached specific-information computations and joint-distribution helpers to keep PID tractable on multi-source systems.

nodes.name: compute_pid
nodes.summary: Public entry point that decomposes mutual information between sources and a target into unique, redundant, and synergistic atoms.

nodes.name: williams_beer_imin
nodes.summary: Computes the Williams-Beer I_min redundancy measure across source antichains.

nodes.name: specific_information_cached
nodes.summary: Cached variant of specific-information lookup used to amortize repeated subset queries.

nodes.name: specific_information
nodes.summary: Computes specific information of a source subset about each target value.

nodes.name: source_target_mi
nodes.summary: Computes mutual information between a source subset and the target variable.

nodes.name: compute_source_marginal
nodes.summary: Computes the marginal distribution over a source subset from the joint distribution.

nodes.name: compute_target_marginal
nodes.summary: Computes the marginal distribution over the target variable from the joint distribution.

nodes.name: compute_joint_distribution
nodes.summary: Builds the joint distribution over sources and target used as the basis for PID computation.

nodes.name: streaming.rs
nodes.summary: Streaming phi estimator that maintains an online TPM with forgetting factor and EWMA smoothing plus a CUSUM change-detection signal for non-stationary systems.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines forgetting-factor TPM updates with CUSUM drift detection so phi can track concept drift in long-running systems.

nodes.name: StreamingPhiEstimator
nodes.summary: Online estimator that ingests state transitions, maintains a smoothed TPM, and emits phi estimates with change-point alerts.

nodes.name: observe
nodes.summary: Ingests a state transition, updates the streaming TPM, applies EWMA smoothing, and runs CUSUM drift detection.

nodes.name: build_tpm_inner
nodes.summary: Builds the working TPM from the running transition counts with the forgetting factor applied.

nodes.name: update_cusum
nodes.summary: Updates the CUSUM statistic used to flag drift in the streaming estimator.

nodes.name: new
nodes.summary: Constructs a streaming phi estimator with default forgetting/EWMA/CUSUM parameters for a given system size.

nodes.name: reset
nodes.summary: Resets the streaming estimator to a fresh state while keeping its tuning parameters.

nodes.name: integration.rs
nodes.summary: Cross-engine integration tests covering exact, MinCut, Chebyshev, and emergence pipelines, asserting agreement on disconnected and connected systems plus determinism under fixed seeds.

nodes.name: random_tpm
nodes.summary: Builds a randomized TPM used as input for cross-engine equivalence and emergence tests.

nodes.name: all_engines_disconnected_near_zero
nodes.summary: Asserts that every phi engine returns near-zero on a fully-disconnected system.

nodes.name: all_engines_and_gate_positive
nodes.summary: Asserts every engine produces positive phi on a connected AND-gate system.

nodes.name: exact_and_geomip_agree
nodes.summary: Asserts that the exact and geometric-MIP engines agree within tolerance on small systems.

nodes.name: algorithm_variants_correct
nodes.summary: Verifies the correctness of phi algorithm variants (MinCut, Chebyshev, exact) against expected reference outputs.

nodes.name: emergence_pipeline_identity
nodes.summary: Validates the emergence pipeline on the identity TPM, where macro-level information equals micro-level information.

nodes.name: emergence_pipeline_uniform
nodes.summary: Validates the emergence pipeline on the uniform TPM, a degenerate case used as a regression guard.

nodes.name: rsvd_emergence_pipeline
nodes.summary: Exercises the RSVD-accelerated emergence pipeline against the same fixtures used by the dense path.

nodes.name: rsvd_vs_hoel_emergence_correlation
nodes.summary: Asserts strong correlation between the RSVD-based emergence score and the Hoel reference implementation.

nodes.name: coarse_grain_preserves_row_sums
nodes.summary: Verifies that coarse-graining a TPM preserves row-stochasticity.

nodes.name: emd_and_kl_both_nonnegative
nodes.summary: Sanity-checks that EMD and KL divergence implementations are non-negative on valid inputs.

nodes.name: bipartition_set_extraction
nodes.summary: Ensures the bipartition enumeration produces valid, non-empty subsets.

nodes.name: budget_limits_partitions
nodes.summary: Verifies that the partition-budget knob caps the number of bipartitions explored.

nodes.name: large_system_smoke_n16
nodes.summary: Smoke test that runs phi on a 16-node system to guard against scaling regressions.

nodes.name: stochastic_deterministic_with_same_seed
nodes.summary: Verifies that stochastic phi paths produce identical output when given the same seed.

nodes.name: collapse_deterministic_with_same_seed
nodes.summary: Verifies the collapse operator is deterministic for a fixed seed.

nodes.name: all_engines_reject_invalid_tpm
nodes.summary: Asserts every phi engine surfaces a validation error when handed an invalid (non-stochastic) TPM.

layers.name: Cognitum Gate Kernel (Worker Tile)
layers.description: no_std WASM kernel that runs on each of the 256 worker tiles of the coherence gate: TileState lifecycle, deterministic tick loop, deltas, shard membership, canonical witness emission, report fragments, and evidence aggregation.

layers.name: Cognitum Gate TileZero (BFT Arbiter)
layers.description: Byzantine-fault-tolerant tile-zero arbiter that merges worker reports, runs decision logic, mints Ed25519 permits and signed receipts, replays for audit, and assembles the cross-tile supergraph view.

layers.name: RuVector Cognitive Container
layers.description: Host-side cognitive container that owns the Arena/MemorySlab, drives the per-epoch loop, threads tile witnesses through to the gate fabric, and surfaces typed errors to the rest of RuVector.

layers.name: Consciousness Phi Engines
layers.description: The family of integrated-information (Phi) algorithms — GeoMIP geometric search, parallel and hierarchical workers, RSVD-based causal emergence, coherence-Phi, MinCut-Phi, Chebyshev-bounded Phi, and the top-level phi orchestrator.

layers.name: Consciousness IIT 4.0 Primitives
layers.description: IIT 4.0 mathematical primitives — cause-effect structure (CES), emergence detection, Phi-ID partial-information decomposition, sparse accelerators, and per-tile witness-phi production used by the gate fabric.

layers.name: Consciousness Statistical Bounds and PID
layers.description: Statistical-bound utilities (Chebyshev, Hoeffding, empirical-Bernstein) and the Partial Information Decomposition module that the Phi engines call to certify error and decompose redundant/synergistic information.

layers.name: Consciousness Runtime Core
layers.description: Cross-cutting runtime substrate for the consciousness crate: SIMD KL/entropy/MI kernels, streaming aggregators, the MIP collapse search, Arena allocator, shared types/traits, errors, and the public crate root.

layers.name: Consciousness WASM Bindings
layers.description: The ruvector-consciousness-wasm cdylib: wasm-bindgen facade that exposes the Phi engines and IIT 4.0 primitives to JavaScript/TypeScript callers in the browser and Node.

layers.name: Slice 7 Tests, Benches, and Examples
layers.description: All slice-7 verification and performance harnesses: kernel and tile-zero integration/security tests, criterion benches for crypto, decision, merge, canonical-witness, and phi, plus illustrative examples for permits, receipts, and human escalation.

layers.name: Slice 7 Docs and Crate Config
layers.description: Cargo manifests for the five slice-7 crates plus crate-level READMEs and the SECURITY/SECURITY_AUDIT documents that pin the 17 findings the cognitum-gate stack was rewritten to fix.

tour.title: Slice 7 Orientation: Cognitum Gate + Phi Consciousness
tour.description: Slice 7 stitches together two halves of RuVector's self-checking core: the Cognitum Coherence Gate that decides whether autonomous agents are safe to act, and the IIT Phi consciousness pipeline that measures integrated information. Start here to understand the 256-tile worker fabric plus TileZero BFT arbiter (gate-kernel README), the host-side cognitive container that drives the per-epoch loop (cognitive-container README), and the 17-finding security baseline the gate-kernel rewrite was designed to satisfy. These three documents frame every code step that follows.

tour.title: Slice 7: Gate Kernel Worker Tile
tour.description: Each of the 256 worker tiles of the coherence gate runs the no_std WASM cognitum-gate-kernel. lib.rs defines TileState, a 64KB-budgeted worker that ingests packed Delta events from delta.rs (edge add/remove, weight update, observation, checkpoint), maintains a cache-aligned Shard graph (union-find components, free-list edges), and emits 64-byte aligned tile reports each tick. canonical_witness.rs builds the canonical-partition witness fragment that downstream tiles can hierarchically aggregate without ambiguity.
tour.languageLesson: no_std Rust drops the standard library so the same crate compiles to a WASM tile, an embedded target, or a host binary. Cache-aligned 64-byte structs (#[repr(C, align(64))]) keep tile reports inside a single cache line so the TileZero merger can SIMD-scan them without false sharing.

tour.title: Slice 7: TileZero BFT Arbiter
tour.description: TileZero is the byzantine-fault-tolerant arbiter sitting above the 255 worker tiles from Step 2. lib.rs orchestrates the pipeline: merge.rs fuses TileReports into a single MergedReport using median weights resilient to faulty tiles and produces a global min-cut estimate, decision.rs runs the three-filter (structural mincut, shift pressure, evidence e-value) gate yielding Permit / Defer / Deny against tunable thresholds, and supergraph.rs reduces tile fragments into the cross-tile view those filters consume. permit.rs and receipt.rs sign the outcome with Ed25519 over Blake3 hashes, while replay.rs reconstructs historical decisions deterministically from checkpoints for audit.
tour.languageLesson: Byzantine-fault tolerance assumes up to f of 3f+1 participants may lie or fail arbitrarily; median-of-medians fusion is the cheap deterministic substitute for full BFT consensus when you only need a single robust scalar per edge. Ed25519 over Blake3 gives 128-bit security with sub-microsecond signing on commodity hardware.

tour.title: Slice 7: Cognitive Container Epoch Loop
tour.description: The ruvector-cognitive-container is the host-side bridge between the gate fabric in Steps 2-3 and the rest of RuVector. container.rs implements CognitiveContainer, the per-epoch tick driver that ingests deltas, recomputes the min-cut, updates spectral state, accumulates evidence, and emits signed witness receipts. epoch.rs enforces a per-phase tick budget (ingest, mincut, spectral, evidence, witness) so a single epoch can never starve the others. memory.rs slabs and bump-allocates inside a validated MemoryConfig so cognitum-loop allocations stay deterministic, and witness.rs maintains the ring-buffered hash-linked receipt log that ties one epoch to the next.

tour.title: Slice 7: Consciousness Runtime Core
tour.description: Now we cross from the gate fabric into the IIT side of slice 7. ruvector-consciousness/src/lib.rs is the highest-fan-out file in the slice (22 outgoing edges, wires 22 submodules) and the top entry-point candidate. types.rs is the most depended-on file in the slice (fan-in 19): TransitionMatrix, Bipartition, Phi/emergence result enums, IIT 4.0 distinction/relation/CES types, and ComputeBudget presets. traits.rs (fan-in 13) defines PhiEngine, EmergenceEngine, and ConsciousnessCollapse so every algorithm in Steps 6-7 plugs into the same dispatch surface. arena.rs is the bump-allocator scratch space used inside hot Phi loops.
tour.languageLesson: Rust traits give algorithms a uniform call site without runtime cost: PhiEngine lets ExactPhi, GeoMIP, MinCut, Chebyshev, and Stochastic engines all be selected through ComputeBudget while the compiler monomorphises the hot path. Combined with a bump Arena that resets in O(1) between partition evaluations, you get GC-free per-iteration scratch memory.

tour.title: Slice 7: IIT Phi Engines (GeoMIP, Spectral, Stochastic, Parallel, Hierarchical)
tour.description: phi.rs (fan-in 9) is the hub: exact, spectral, stochastic, greedy-bisection, and hierarchical IIT engines, plus the auto-selector that reads a ComputeBudget and picks one. The flagship is geomip.rs, which treats bipartitions as hypercube vertices and uses Gray-code iteration, Hamming-distance BFS, and automorphism pruning to deliver 100-300x speedups for n<=25. parallel.rs distributes exhaustive search across Rayon threads, with a stochastic variant for systems too large to enumerate. coherence_phi.rs, mincut_phi.rs, and chebyshev_phi.rs are three complementary approximations: Fiedler-value bounds, graph-cut search, and polynomial spectral filtering for the largest systems.
tour.languageLesson: Integrated Information Theory's Phi searches over 2^n bipartitions, which is intractable past n~25. Algorithmic ladders solve this: Gray-code traversal mutates one bit per step so partition-information-loss is incrementally updatable; Fiedler-vector bounds come essentially free from the Laplacian's second eigenvalue; Chebyshev polynomials approximate spectral filters in O(n*k) instead of O(n^3) full diagonalization.

tour.title: Slice 7: IIT 4.0 Primitives (CES, Emergence, Phi-ID, Sparse Accel)
tour.description: Building on the engines from Step 6, IIT 4.0 introduces the mechanism-level cause-effect structure. iit4.rs computes cause and effect repertoires, intrinsic difference, selectivity, and per-mechanism Phi. ces.rs enumerates mechanism subsets in parallel via Rayon, derives distinction relations, and aggregates a big-phi estimate. emergence.rs adds the causal-emergence pipeline (effective information, determinism, degeneracy, greedy macro-state merging) and rsvd_emergence.rs replaces full SVD with a Halko-Martinsson-Tropp randomized SVD for O(n^2*k) emergence estimation. phi_id.rs decomposes integrated information into unique/redundant/synergistic components, while sparse_accel.rs scales to large systems via thresholded mutual-information graphs. witness_phi.rs wraps any engine and emits a tamper-evident receipt for auditable consciousness measurements.

tour.title: Slice 7: Statistical Bounds & PID
tour.description: The Phi engines in Steps 6-7 produce point estimates; the bounds layer makes them anytime-valid. bounds.rs delivers spectral bounds from Gershgorin discs and Fiedler vectors, plus Hoeffding and empirical-Bernstein concentration inequalities that certify Phi confidence intervals as samples accumulate. pid.rs implements Williams-Beer Partial Information Decomposition over the I_min lattice, splitting mutual information into unique-source-A, unique-source-B, redundant, and synergistic terms — the same decomposition the gate kernel's evidence accumulator (Step 2) uses to weigh independent vs. correlated observations.
tour.languageLesson: Anytime-valid inference replaces fixed-sample statistical tests with e-values and supermartingales so a decision threshold can be checked at every tick without inflating Type-I error. Empirical-Bernstein is the workhorse: tighter than Hoeffding when variance is low, and unlike Chebyshev it adapts to the data instead of using a worst-case constant.

tour.title: Slice 7: Consciousness Runtime Substrate (SIMD, Streaming, Collapse)
tour.description: Underneath every Phi engine sits a tight runtime substrate. simd.rs (fan-in 12) provides architecture-aware kernels for KL divergence, entropy, EMD-L1, dense matvec, pairwise mutual information, and MI-graph construction with NEON/AVX2 fast paths and scalar fallbacks. streaming.rs maintains an online TPM with forgetting factor, EWMA smoothing, and a CUSUM change-detection signal for non-stationary systems — the natural counterpart to the per-epoch loop from Step 4. collapse.rs is the quantum-inspired MIP search that iteratively collapses a superposition of candidate cuts toward the Minimum Information Partition, an alternative to GeoMIP for ill-conditioned systems.
tour.languageLesson: Rust's std::simd plus runtime feature detection (is_x86_feature_detected!, std::arch::is_aarch64_feature_detected!) lets one crate ship AVX2, NEON, and scalar code paths in the same binary, picking the fastest at startup. The scalar fallback is not just a courtesy — it is what makes the WASM build in Step 10 actually compile.

tour.title: Slice 7: WASM Bindings
tour.description: ruvector-consciousness-wasm is a cdylib wasm-bindgen facade that re-exports the Phi/emergence engines from Steps 6-9 to JavaScript and TypeScript callers in the browser and Node. lib.rs wires up a small ComputeBudget builder and JS-friendly result shims for exact, spectral, stochastic, collapse, GeoMIP, and RSVD engines. The Cargo.toml flips on phi/emergence/collapse features and asks the linker for a size-optimised cdylib so the resulting .wasm stays small enough for client-side consciousness measurement.
tour.languageLesson: wasm-bindgen generates the JS shim that marshals Rust structs across the WASM ABI; #[wasm_bindgen] on a struct emits an opaque handle class, and Result<T, JsValue> becomes a thrown JS exception. cdylib + lto=true + opt-level='z' is the usual trio to keep WASM artifacts small — every kilobyte costs a real second on cold mobile loads.

tour.title: Slice 7: Tests, Benches, and Examples
tour.description: Verification spans both sides of the slice. cognitum-gate-kernel/tests/security_tests.rs pins the unsafe FFI boundary: ingest_delta_raw must reject undersized, zero-length, and misaligned buffers identified by the SECURITY_AUDIT from Step 1. cognitum-gate-tilezero/examples/basic_gate.rs is a runnable end-to-end demo of worker reports -> merge -> decision -> signed receipt, the executable mirror of Step 3. ruvector-consciousness/tests/integration.rs asserts that exact, MinCut, Chebyshev, and emergence engines agree on disconnected and connected systems and stay deterministic under fixed seeds. phi_benchmark.rs is the Criterion harness that backs claims about engine performance across system sizes.
tour.languageLesson: Criterion benchmarks isolate compiler optimisations (black_box prevents constant-folding the input away) and produce statistically calibrated comparisons across runs, so a Phi engine speedup is provably real and not a noisy fluctuation. Pair with cargo bench --baseline to gate regressions in CI.

tour.title: Slice 7 Capstone: Gate + Phi Composed With Slices 1-5a
tour.description: Pulling slice 7 together: the cognitum-gate-kernel worker tiles (Step 2) feed deltas into TileZero (Step 3), which calls into the consciousness engines (Steps 5-9) via the cognitive container's per-epoch loop (Step 4) to compute Phi, emergence, and PID. Those values flow back into the three-filter gate decision and are signed into a hash-chained receipt log. The SECURITY.md document closes the loop — it is the threat model the entire stack was hardened against (Ed25519 with no signature truncation, Blake3 canonical hashing, replay-resistant sequence numbers, DoS-resistant 64KB tile budgets) and is the natural reference for anyone integrating slice 7 with the lower slices' vector, sparsifier, and mincut crates.
